1	# MediaVault API Specification
2	
3	> Interactive Swagger UI: `GET /docs`  
4	> Machine-readable OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec: `GET /openapi.json`
5	
6	All endpoints that require authentication accept a Bearer token in the
7	`Authorization` header:
8	
9	```
10	Authorization: Bearer <token>
11	```
12	
13	---
14	
15	## Device Identity
16	
17	Auth-protected endpoints optionally read the `X-Device-Id` request header to
18	identify the calling device.  When the header is present, the middleware checks
19	the `blacklists` table for an active entry with `target_type = "device"` and
20	`target_id = <header value>`.
21	
22	**Header**
23	
24	| Header | Required | Description |
25	|--------|----------|-------------|
26	| `X-Device-Id` | No | Opaque device identifier (string, max 255 chars). Required for device-level blacklist enforcement to be applied. Requests without the header bypass device blacklist checks. |
27	
28	**Blacklist semantics** — an entry is *active* when:
29	- `start_at <= NOW`
30	- `end_at IS NULL OR end_at > NOW`
31	
32	**Blocked response** (HTTP 403):
33	
34	```json
35	{
36	  "error": "forbidden",
37	  "message": "Device is blacklisted",
38	  "code": "device_blacklisted"
39	}
40	```
41	
42	Endpoints enforced: all routes protected by `@require_auth` (includes `/auth/me`,
43	`/risk/evaluate`, all `/policies/*` write endpoints, and others).
44	
45	---
46	
47	## Auth
48	
49	### `POST /auth/register`
50	
51	Register a new user account.
52	
53	**Request**
54	```json
55	{
56	  "username": "alice",
57	  "email": "alice@example.com",
58	  "password": "Secret123!Pass"
59	}
60	```
61	
62	**Response 201**
63	```json
64	{
65	  "id": 1,
66	  "username": "alice",
67	  "email": "alice@example.com",
68	  "status": "active"
69	}
70	```
71	
72	**Constraints**
73	- `username`: 3–64 characters, unique
74	- `email`: valid email format, unique
75	- `password`: 3–64 characters
76	
77	---
78	
79	### `POST /auth/login`
80

80	
81	Authenticate and receive a session token.
82	
83	**Request**
84	```json
85	{
86	  "username": "alice",
87	  "password": "Secret123!Pass"
88	}
89	```
90	
91	**Response 200**
92	```json
93	{
94	  "token": "<session-token>",
95	  "expires_at": "2026-04-11T12:00:00+00:00"
96	}
97	```
98	
99	**Lockout**: 5 consecutive failures within 15 minutes locks the account for 30 minutes.
100	
101	---
102	
103	### `GET /auth/me`
104	
105	Return the authenticated user's own profile.
106	
107	**Headers**: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, optional `X-Device-Id`
108	
109	**Response 200**
110	```json
111	{
112	  "id": 1,
113	  "username": "alice",
114	  "email": "alice@example.com",
115	  "status": "active",
116	  "roles": ["user"]
117	}
118	```
119	
120	---
121	
122	### `POST /auth/logout`
123	
124	Revoke the current session token.
125	
126	**Headers**: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
127	
128	**Response 200**
129	```json
130	{"message": "Logged out"}
131	```
132	
133	---
134	
135	## Risk
136	
137	### `POST /risk/evaluate`
138	
139	Evaluate a risk signal for an event.
140	
141	**Headers**: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, optional `X-Device-Id`
142	
143	**Request**
144	```json
145	{
146	  "event_type": "login",
147	  "ip": "1.2.3.4",
148	  "device_id": "device-abc-123",
149	  "user_id": 42
150	}
151	```
152	
153	| Field | Required | Description |
154	|-------|----------|-------------|
155	| `event_type` | Yes | e.g. `login`, `registration`, `reserve`, `coupon_apply`, `profile_edit` |
156	| `ip` | No | Client IP address |
157	| `device_id` | No | Device identifier (also checked via `X-Device-Id` header in auth middleware) |
158	| `user_id` | No | Target user ID |
159	
160	**Response 200**
161	```json
162	{
163	  "decision": "deny",
164	  "reasons": ["rapid_account_creation"],
165	  "event_id": 99
166	}
167	```
168	
169	`decision` values: `allow`, `challenge`, `throttle`, `deny`
170	
171	---
172	
173	### Blacklist CRUD
174	
175	#### `POST /risk/blacklist`
176	
177	Create a blacklist entry. Admin only.
178	
179	**Request**
180	```json
181	{
182	  "target_type": "device",
183	  "target_id": "device-abc-123",
184	  "reason": "Suspicious activity",
185	  "start_at": "2026-04-10T00:00:00Z",
186	  "end_at": null
187	}
188	```
189	
190	`target_type` values: `user`, `device`, `ip`
191	
192	---
193	
194	## Policies
195	
196	### Allowed Policy Types
197	
198	`policy_type` must be one of the following enumerated values.  Unknown values
199	are rejected with **422 Unprocessable Entity**.
200	
201	| Type | Description |
202	|------|-------------|
203	| `booking` | Booking concurrency and session rules |
204	| `course_selection` | Course selection limits |
205	| `warehouse_ops` | Warehouse operation thresholds |
206	| `pricing` | Pricing configuration |
207	| `risk` | Risk signal thresholds |
208	| `rate_limit` | API rate-limit configuration |
209	| `membership` | Membership tier configuration |
210	| `coupon` | Coupon discount limits |
211	
212	---
213	
214	### `POST /policies`
215	
216	Create a policy in `draft` status. Admin only.
217	
218	**Request**
219	```json
220	{
221	  "policy_type": "risk",
222	  "name": "Production Risk Policy",
223	  "semver": "1.0.0",
224	  "effective_from": "2026-01-01T00:00:00",
225	  "rules_json": "{\"rapid_account_creation_threshold\": 5}",
226	  "effective_until": null,
227	  "description": "Initial risk thresholds"
228	}
229	```
230	
231	**Response 201** — returns the policy object with `"status": "draft"`.
232	
233	**422** is returned when `policy_type` is not in the allowlist.
234	
235	---
236	
237	### `GET /policies/resolve`
238	
239	Resolve the effective policy rules for a given user. Admin only.
240	
241	**Query parameters**
242	
243	| Parameter | Required | Description |
244	|-----------|----------|-------------|
245	| `policy_type` | Yes | One of the allowed policy types |
246	| `user_id` | Yes | Integer user ID for canary bucketing |
247	| `segment` | No | Caller segment string (e.g. `beta_users`) for segment-aware rollout resolution |
248	
249	**Canary rollout semantics**
250	
251	When a `PolicyRollout` row exists for the active policy, deterministic
252	hash-bucketing (`MD5(user_id) % 100 < rollout_pct`) is used to decide whether
253	this user is in the canary cohort.
254	
255	**Segment precedence (highest to lowest)**:
256	
257	1. Segment-specific rollout: `PolicyRollout.segment == requested segment` (exact match)
258	2. Global rollout: `PolicyRollout.segment IS NULL`
259	3. No rollout → the entire active policy applies to every user
260	
261	A user whose segment does not match any segment-specific rollout and there is no
262	global rollout receives the full active (non-canary) policy.
263	
264	**Example — user in beta_users segment, canary at 100%**
265	
266	```
267	GET /policies/resolve?policy_type=pricing&user_id=42&segment=beta_users
268	Authorization: Bearer <admin-token>
269	```
270	
271	**Response 200**
272	```json
273	{
274	  "rules_json": {
275	    "base_price_cents": 2000
276	  }
277	}
278	```
279	
280	---
281	
282	### `POST /policies/{id}/canary`
283	
284	Create a canary rollout for a policy. Admin only.
285	
286	**Request**
287	```json
288	{
289	  "rollout_pct": 20,
290	  "segment": "beta_users"
291	}
292	```
293	
294	`segment` is optional. Omit (or send `null`) for a global rollout that applies
295	to users regardless of segment.
296	
297	---
298	
299	## Profiles
300	
301	### `GET /profiles/{id}`
302	
303	Return a user's public profile. Authenticated users only.
304	
305	**Response 200**
306	```json
307	{
308	  "id": 1,
309	  "user_id": 1,
310	  "bio": "...",
311	  "visibility": "public"
312	}
313	```
314	
315	`visibility` values: `public`, `private`
316	
317	Profiles in a `VisibilityGroup` are only visible to members of that group.
318	
319	---
320	
321	## Compliance
322	
323	### `POST /compliance/export-request`
324	
325	Submit a GDPR data export request for the authenticated user.
326	
327	**Headers**: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
328	
329	**Request body**: empty or `{}`
330	
331	**Response 201**
332	```json
333	{
334	  "id": 7,
335	  "type": "export",
336	  "status": "pending",
337	  "user_id": 42,
338	  "created_at": "2026-04-10T09:00:00+00:00"
339	}
340	```
341	
342	---
343	
344	### `POST /compliance/export-request/{id}/process`
345	
346	Process a pending export request and write the export file. Admin only.
347	
348	**Response 200**
349	```json
350	{
351	  "id": 7,
352	  "status": "complete",
353	  "export_path": "/app/data/exports/7.json"
354	}
355	```
356	
357	---
358	
359	### `POST /compliance/deletion-request`
360	
361	Submit a GDPR deletion request for the authenticated user.
362	
363	**Request body**: empty or `{}`
364	
365	**Response 201**
366	```json
367	{
368	  "id": 8,
369	  "type": "deletion",
370	  "status": "pending",
371	  "user_id": 42
372	}
373	```
374	
375	---
376	
377	### `POST /compliance/deletion-request/{id}/process`
378	
379	Process a deletion request: anonymise PII, revoke sessions. Admin only.  
380	Ledger entries are reassigned to the sentinel user (7-year retention) and
381	are never hard-deleted.
382	
383	---
384	
385	## Error Response Shape
386	
387	All error responses use a consistent JSON envelope:
388	
389	```json
390	{
391	  "error": "<machine_readable_code>",
392	  "message": "<human_readable_description>",
393	  "code": "<optional_sub_code>"
394	}
395	```
396	
397	| HTTP Status | `error` value |
398	|-------------|---------------|
399	| 400 | `bad_request` |
400	| 401 | `unauthorized` |
401	| 403 | `forbidden` |
402	| 404 | `not_found` |
403	| 409 | `conflict` |
404	| 422 | `unprocessable_entity` |
405	| 429 | `too_many_requests` |
406	| 500 | `internal_server_error` |
407	
408	Device blacklist 403 additionally includes `"code": "device_blacklisted"`.
409

80	
81	    if scope == "mutual_followers":
82	        a_follows_b = ProfileFollow.query.filter_by(
83	            follower_id=requesting_user_id, followee_id=target_user_id
84	        ).first()
85	        b_follows_a = ProfileFollow.query.filter_by(
86	            follower_id=target_user_id, followee_id=requesting_user_id
87	        ).first()
88	        if a_follows_b and b_follows_a:

91	
92	    if scope == "custom_group":

98	            if member:

100	        return _stub_profile(profile)
101	
102	    # Fallback
103	    return _full_profile(profile)
104	
105	
106	def update_profile(user_id: int, **kwargs) -> Profile:
107	    """Update own profile. Validates bio max 500 chars."""
108	    profile = Profile.query.filter_by(user_id=user_id).first()
109	    if profile is None:
110	        raise LookupError("profile_not_found")
111	
112	    bio = kwargs.get("bio")
113	    if bio is not None and len(bio) > 500:
114	        raise ValueError("bio_too_long")
115	
116	    allowed = {
117	        "display_name", "bio", "interest_tags_json",
118	        "media_references_json", "visibility_scope", "visibility_group_id",
119	    }
120	    for key, value in kwargs.items():
121	        if key in allowed:
122	            setattr(profile, key, value)
123	
124	    db.session.commit()
125	    return profile
126	
127	
128	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
129	# Visibility Groups
130	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
131	
132	def create_visibility_group(owner_id: int, name: str, member_ids: list) -> VisibilityGroup:
133	    """Create a visibility group with the given members."""
134	    group = VisibilityGroup(owner_id=owner_id, name=name)
135	    db.session.add(group)
136	    db.session.flush()
137	
138	    for uid in member_ids:
139	        member = VisibilityGroupMember(group_id=group.id, user_id=uid)
140	        db.session.add(member)
141	
142	    db.session.commit()
143	    return group
144	
145	
146	def get_visibility_group(group_id: int, requesting_user_id: int = None) -> dict:
147	    """Return group info with members list.
148	
149	    Access policy: only the group owner and current members may read the group.
150	    Raises PermissionError if requesting_user_id is provided and is neither
151	    the owner nor a member.
152	    """
153	    group = db.session.get(VisibilityGroup, group_id)
154	    if group is None:
155	        raise LookupError("group_not_found")
156	
157	    if requesting_user_id is not None:
158	        is_owner = group.owner_id == requesting_user_id
159	        is_member = (
160	            VisibilityGroupMember.query
161	            .filter_by(group_id=group_id, user_id=requesting_user_id)
162	            .first()
163	        ) is not None
164	        if not is_owner and not is_member:
165	            raise PermissionError("forbidden")
166	
167	    members = VisibilityGroupMember.query.filter_by(group_id=group_id).all()
168	    return {
169	        "id": group.id,
170	        "name": group.name,
171	        "owner_id": group.owner_id,
172	        "members": [m.user_id for m in members],
173	    }
174	
175	
176	def add_group_member(group_id: int, user_id: int, actor_id: int) -> VisibilityGroupMember:
177	    """Add a member to a visibility group. Actor must be the owner."""
178	    group = db.session.get(VisibilityGroup, group_id)
179	    if group is None:
180	        raise LookupError("group_not_found")
181	    if group.owner_id != actor_id:
182	        raise PermissionError("not_group_owner")
183	    try:
184	        member = VisibilityGroupMember(group_id=group_id, user_id=user_id)
185	        db.session.add(member)
186	        db.session.commit()
187	    except IntegrityError:
188	        db.session.rollback()
189	        raise ValueError("already_member")
190	    return member
191	
192	
193	def remove_group_member(group_id: int, user_id: int, actor_id: int) -> None:
194	    """Remove a member from a visibility group. Actor must be the owner."""
195	    group = db.session.get(VisibilityGroup, group_id)
196	    if group is None:
197	        raise LookupError("group_not_found")
198	    if group.owner_id != actor_id:
199	        raise PermissionError("not_group_owner")
200	    member = VisibilityGroupMember.query.filter_by(group_id=group_id, user_id=user_id).first()
201	    if member is None:
202	        raise LookupError("member_not_found")
203	    db.session.delete(member)
204	    db.session.commit()
205	
206	
207	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
208	# Follow / Unfollow
209	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
210	
211	def follow_user(follower_id: int, followee_id: int) -> ProfileFollow:
212	    """Create a follow relationship. Raises PermissionError if blocked."""
213	    if is_blocked(follower_id, followee_id):
214	        raise PermissionError("Access denied")
215	    try:
216	        follow = ProfileFollow(follower_id=follower_id, followee_id=followee_id)
217	        db.session.add(follow)
218	        db.session.commit()
219	    except IntegrityError:
220	        db.session.rollback()
221	        raise ValueError("already_following")
222	    return follow
223	
224	
225	def unfollow_user(follower_id: int, followee_id: int) -> None:
226	    """Remove a follow relationship."""
227	    follow = ProfileFollow.query.filter_by(
228	        follower_id=follower_id, followee_id=followee_id
229	    ).first()
230	    if follow is None:
231	        raise LookupError("follow_not_found")
232	    db.session.delete(follow)
233	    db.session.commit()
234	
235	
236	def get_followers(user_id: int) -> list:
237	    """Return user_ids that follow this user."""
238	    follows = ProfileFollow.query.filter_by(followee_id=user_id).all()
239	    return [f.follower_id for f in follows]
240	
241	
242	def get_following(user_id: int) -> list:
243	    """Return user_ids that this user follows."""
244	    follows = ProfileFollow.query.filter_by(follower_id=user_id).all()
245	    return [f.followee_id for f in follows]
246

248	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
249	# Block / Unblock
250	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
251	
252	def block_user(blocker_id: int, blocked_id: int) -> ProfileBlock:
253	    """Block a user. Remove any existing follow relationships in both directions."""
254	    # Remove follow in both directions
255	    for follow in ProfileFollow.query.filter(
256	        db.or_(
257	            db.and_(ProfileFollow.follower_id == blocker_id, ProfileFollow.followee_id == blocked_id),
258	            db.and_(ProfileFollow.follower_id == blocked_id, ProfileFollow.followee_id == blocker_id),
259	        )
260	    ).all():
261	        db.session.delete(follow)
262	
263	    try:
264	        block = ProfileBlock(blocker_id=blocker_id, blocked_id=blocked_id)
265	        db.session.add(block)
266	        db.session.commit()
267	    except IntegrityError:
268	        db.session.rollback()
269	        raise ValueError("already_blocked")
270	    return block
271	
272	
273	def unblock_user(blocker_id: int, blocked_id: int) -> None:
274	    """Unblock a user."""
275	    block = ProfileBlock.query.filter_by(blocker_id=blocker_id, blocked_id=blocked_id).first()
276	    if block is None:
277	        raise LookupError("block_not_found")
278	    db.session.delete(block)
279	    db.session.commit()

283	# Hide

285	
286	def hide_user(hider_id: int, hidden_id: int) -> ProfileHide:
287	    """Hide a user from feed."""
288	    try:
289	        hide = ProfileHide(hider_id=hider_id, hidden_id=hidden_id)
290	        db.session.add(hide)
291	        db.session.commit()
292	    except IntegrityError:
293	        db.session.rollback()
294	        raise ValueError("already_hidden")

16	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	# Retention policy

19	
20	# Financial / ledger records must be retained for this many years regardless
21	# of any user deletion request (regulatory requirement).
22	LEDGER_RETENTION_YEARS = 7
23	
24	
25	def is_within_retention_window(created_at: datetime) -> bool:
26	    """Return True if *created_at* falls within the mandatory retention window.
27	
28	    Ledger entries created within the last LEDGER_RETENTION_YEARS years must
29	    not be hard-deleted; they are instead reassigned to the sentinel user so
30	    financial integrity is preserved while PII is removed.
31	    """
32	    if created_at is None:
33	        return True  # conservatively retain records with unknown creation dates
34	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
35	    cutoff = now - timedelta(days=365 * LEDGER_RETENTION_YEARS)
36	    created_aware = created_at if created_at.tzinfo else created_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
37	    return created_aware > cutoff

Revision ID: 0002
Revises: 0001
Create Date: 2026-04-10 00:00:00.000000

Rationale
---------
policy_type was previously an unconstrained VARCHAR.  This migration adds a
DB-level CHECK constraint so that any direct INSERT or UPDATE that violates
the allowed set is rejected by the database engine itself, providing a second
layer of defence in addition to the service- and schema-level validation.

SQLite note
-----------
SQLite does not support ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT.  Alembic's
``batch_alter_table`` context manager works around this by copying the table
into a new table with the constraint, then renaming it back.  The operation
is safe when data already satisfies the constraint (all existing rows were
written through the service-layer allowlist).
"""
from typing import Sequence, Union

from alembic import op


revision: str = "0002"
down_revision: Union[str, None] = "0001"
branch_labels: Union[str, Sequence[str], None] = None
depends_on: Union[str, Sequence[str], None] = None

    "booking",
    "course_selection",
    "warehouse_ops",
    "pricing",
    "risk",
    "rate_limit",
    "membership",
    "coupon",
)
_CHECK_SQL = "policy_type IN ({})".format(",".join(f"'{t}'" for t in _ALLOWED))


def upgrade() -> None:
    with op.batch_alter_table("policies", schema=None) as batch_op:
        batch_op.create_check_constraint(
            "ck_policies_policy_type",
            _CHECK_SQL,
        )


def downgrade() -> None:
    with op.batch_alter_table("policies", schema=None) as batch_op:
        batch_op.drop_constraint("ck_policies_policy_type", type_="check")
"}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Schema-layer enum validation (marshmallow OneOf)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_schema_rejects_unknown_type_with_422(client, admin_token):
    """Unknown policy_type is rejected at schema (marshmallow OneOf) level before
    reaching the service, producing 422 with 'invalid_policy_type' in the message."""
    resp = client.post(
        "/policies",
        json={
            "policy_type": "totally_unknown_type",
            "name": "Should fail",
            "semver": "1.0.0",
            "effective_from": "2026-01-01T00:00:00",
            "rules_json": "{}",
        },
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    data = resp.get_json()
    assert data["error"] == "unprocessable_entity"
    # The OneOf error message is embedded in the response message
    assert "invalid_policy_type" in data["message"]


def test_schema_rejects_empty_type_with_422(client, admin_token):
    """Empty string is not a valid policy_type; rejected at schema level."""
    resp = client.post(
        "/policies",
        json={
            "policy_type": "",
            "name": "Empty type",
            "semver": "1.0.0",
            "effective_from": "2026-01-01T00:00:00",
            "rules_json": "{}",
        },
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DB-layer check constraint
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_db_check_constraint_rejects_invalid_type(app):
    """Direct DB insert with an invalid policy_type must be rejected by the
    CHECK constraint, raising IntegrityError (or OperationalError on SQLite)."""
    from datetime import datetime, timezone
    from app.models.policy import Policy
    from app.extensions import db

    with app.app_context():
        bad = Policy(
            policy_type="not_allowed",
            name="Constraint Test",
            semver="99.0.0",
            effective_from=datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            rules_json="{}",
            status="draft",
        )
        db.session.add(bad)
        with pytest.raises(
            (sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError, sqlalchemy.exc.OperationalError),
            match=r"(?i)(constraint|check|integrity)",
        ):
            db.session.commit()
        db.session.rollback()


def test_db_check_constraint_allows_valid_types(app):
    """Direct DB insert of every allowed policy_type must succeed (no constraint error)."""
    from datetime import datetime, timezone
    from app.models.policy import Policy, _ALLOWED_POLICY_TYPES_SQL
    from app.extensions import db

    with app.app_context():
        for ptype in _ALLOWED_POLICY_TYPES_SQL:
            row = Policy(
                policy_type=ptype,
                name=f"DB Constraint OK {ptype}",
                semver="99.0.1",
                effective_from=datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
                rules_json="{}",
                status="draft",
            )
            db.session.add(row)
        # Should commit without any constraint error
        db.session.commit()"}

        json={

            "name": "Empty type",
            "semver": "1.0.0",
            "effective_from": "2026-01-01T00:00:00",
            "rules_json": "{}",
        },
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DB-layer check constraint
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_db_check_constraint_rejects_invalid_type(app):
    """Direct DB insert with an invalid policy_type must be rejected by the
    CHECK constraint, raising IntegrityError (or OperationalError on SQLite)."""
    from datetime import datetime, timezone
    from app.models.policy import Policy
    from app.extensions import db

    with app.app_context():
        bad = Policy(
            policy_type="not_allowed",
            name="Constraint Test",
            semver="99.0.0",
            effective_from=datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            rules_json="{}",
            status="draft",
        )
        db.session.add(bad)
        with pytest.raises(
            (sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError, sqlalchemy.exc.OperationalError),
            match=r"(?i)(constraint|check|integrity)",
        ):
            db.session.commit()
        db.session.rollback()


def test_db_check_constraint_allows_valid_types(app):
    """Direct DB insert of every allowed policy_type must succeed (no constraint error)."""
    from datetime import datetime, timezone
    from app.models.policy import Policy, _ALLOWED_POLICY_TYPES_SQL
    from app.extensions import db

    with app.app_context():
        for ptype in _ALLOWED_POLICY_TYPES_SQL:
            row = Policy(
                policy_type=ptype,
                name=f"DB Constraint OK {ptype}",
                semver="99.0.1",
                effective_from=datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
                rules_json="{}",
                status="draft",
            )
            db.session.add(row)
        # Should commit without any constraint error
        db.session.commit()"}

### `GET /profiles/{id}`

Return a user's public profile. Authenticated users only.

**Response 200**
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "user_id": 1,
  "bio": "...",
  "visibility": "public"
}
```

`visibility` values: `public`, `private`

Profiles in a `VisibilityGroup` are only visible to members of that group.

---", "new_string": "## Profiles

### `GET /profiles/{id}`

Return a user's profile, filtered by visibility scope. Authenticated users only.

**Response 200** — full profile (when requester has access)
```json
{
  "user_id": 1,
  "display_name": "Alice",
  "bio": "...",
  "interest_tags": null,
  "media_references": null,
  "visibility_scope": "public",
  "visibility_group_id": null
}
```

**Stub profile** (when requester does not meet visibility requirements)
```json
{
  "user_id": 1,
  "display_name": "Alice"
}
```

`visibility_scope` values: `public`, `mutual_followers`, `custom_group`

- `public` — full profile returned to any authenticated user
- `mutual_followers` — full profile only when A follows B and B follows A; otherwise stub
- `custom_group` — full profile only when requester is a member of the profile's visibility group; otherwise stub

**403** is returned when either party has blocked the other.

---

### Visibility Groups

#### `POST /profiles/groups`

Create a visibility group. Authenticated users only. The creator becomes the owner.

**Request**
```json
{"name": "Close Friends", "member_ids": [2, 3]}
```

**Response 201**
```json
{"id": 5, "name": "Close Friends", "owner_id": 1}
```

---

#### `GET /profiles/groups/{id}`

Return group info with member list.

**Access**: owner or any current member. Admins without membership are denied.  
Other users receive **403**.

**Response 200**
```json
{"id": 5, "name": "Close Friends", "owner_id": 1, "members": [2, 3]}
```

---

#### `POST /profiles/groups/{id}/members`

Add a member to a visibility group.

**Access**: **group owner only**. Non-owners (including admins without ownership) receive **403**.

**Request**
```json
{"user_id": 4}
```

**Response 201**
```json
{"message": "member added"}
```

| Status | Condition |
|--------|-----------|
| 201 | Member added successfully |
| 403 | Caller is not the group owner |
| 404 | Group not found |
| 409 | User is already a member |

---

#### `DELETE /profiles/groups/{id}/members/{user_id}`

Remove a member from a visibility group.

**Access**: **group owner only**. Non-owners (including admins without ownership) receive **403**.

**Response 200**
```json
{"message": "member removed"}
```

| Status | Condition |
|--------|-----------|
| 200 | Member removed successfully |
| 403 | Caller is not the group owner |
| 404 | Group or member not found |

---"}

Submit a GDPR data export request for the authenticated user.

**Headers**: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

**Request body**: none required

**Response 201**
```json
{
  "request_id": 7,
  "status": "pending"
}
```

To download the generated file after processing, use
`GET /compliance/export-request/{id}/download`.

---

### `POST /compliance/export-request/{id}/process`

Process a pending export request and write the export JSON file to disk. Admin only.

**Response 200**
```json
{
  "request_id": 7,
  "status": "complete"
}
```

The export file is written server-side; use the `/download` endpoint to retrieve it.  
Note: `export_path` is **not** included in this response.

---

### `GET /compliance/export-request/{id}/download`

Download the generated export file. Owner or admin only.

**Response**: `application/json` file attachment (`export_{id}.json`).

| Status | Condition |
|--------|-----------|
| 200 | File returned as attachment |
| 403 | Requester is neither the request owner nor an admin |
| 404 | Export not found or not yet complete |

---

### `POST /compliance/deletion-request`

Submit a GDPR deletion request for the authenticated user.

**Request body**: none required

**Response 201**
```json
{
  "request_id": 8,
  "status": "pending"
}
```

---

### `POST /compliance/deletion-request/{id}/process`

Process a deletion request: anonymise PII, revoke sessions. Admin only.  
Ledger entries are reassigned to the sentinel user (7-year retention) and
are never hard-deleted.

**Response 200**
```json
{
  "request_id": 8,
  "status": "complete"
}
```"}

**234 passed, 0 failed.** Here is the change summary:

---

## Change Summary

### Issue 1 — `policy_type` constrained at all three layers

**Schema layer** — `app/api/policies.py`
- Added module-level `from app.services.policy_service import ALLOWED_POLICY_TYPES`
- `PolicyCreateSchema.policy_type` now uses `ma.validate.OneOf(sorted(ALLOWED_POLICY_TYPES), error="invalid_policy_type: ...")` — invalid types are rejected with 422 before the handler even runs

**Model/storage layer** — `app/models/policy.py`
- Added `__table_args__` with a `db.CheckConstraint` using an `IN (...)` expression covering all 8 allowed types (name: `ck_policies_policy_type`)
- Fresh `db.create_all()` (tests) picks this up automatically

**Migration** — `migrations/versions/0002_policy_type_check_constraint.py` (new)
- Adds the same check constraint to existing databases via `op.batch_alter_table` (SQLite-compatible table-recreation approach)
- Includes `downgrade()` to drop the constraint

**Error handler** — `app/__init__.py`
- The 422 handler now inspects `e.data["messages"]` / `e.data["errors"]` (flask-smorest/webargs validation errors) and includes them in the response `message` field, rather than only using the generic HTTP description string

**Collateral test fixes** — `tests/test_security_idor.py`
- Both `test_regular_user_cannot_create_policy` and `test_regular_user_cannot_activate_policy` updated to use a valid `policy_type="risk"` with valid `rules_json`, so schema validation passes and the auth/role check (the thing actually being tested) fires

**New tests** — `tests/test_policies.py`
- `test_schema_rejects_unknown_type_with_422` — confirms schema-layer `OneOf` fires, returns 422 with `"invalid_policy_type"` in body
- `test_schema_rejects_empty_type_with_422` — empty string also rejected at schema layer
- `test_db_check_constraint_rejects_invalid_type` — direct DB insert with invalid type raises `IntegrityError` or `OperationalError`
- `test_db_check_constraint_allows_valid_types` — all 8 allowed types inserted directly via ORM without error

---

### Issue 2 — `docs/api-spec.md` aligned with implementation

**`/compliance/export-request` (POST) response** — changed from `{id, type, status, user_id, created_at}` to `{request_id, status}` (matches `create_export_request` return value)

**`/compliance/export-request/{id}/process` (POST) response** — changed from `{id, status, export_path}` to `{request_id, status}` and added note that `export_path` is not included; added entry for the `/download` endpoint

**`/compliance/deletion-request` (POST) response** — changed from `{id, type, status, user_id}` to `{request_id, status}`

**`/compliance/deletion-request/{id}/process` (POST)** — added explicit response body `{request_id, status}`

**`/profiles/{id}` (GET)** — corrected response fields (`user_id`, `display_name`, `bio`, `visibility_scope` etc.) to match `_full_profile()` / `_stub_profile()` actual output; documented stub profile path and all three `visibility_scope` values

**`/profiles/groups` section (new)** — documented all four group endpoints with correct access semantics:
- `GET /profiles/groups/{id}`: **owner or member** (admins without membership are denied)
- `POST /profiles/groups/{id}/members`: **owner only** — explicitly corrects the "owner or admin" misconception
- `DELETE /profiles/groups/{id}/members/{user_id}`: **owner only**
all issues fixed?
